title: Daily Briefing — May 26
headline: Steady grind higher, but sector rotation is getting choppy — flows and 20 MAs are your guide.
body: Right, so today the market’s crawling along with SPY at $745.64 (+0.4%), VIX sinking under the 20-day—volatility is dying and nobody’s panicking. Fear & Greed parked at 58.6: not euphoria, not opportunity. The big picture? Uptrend intact, but momentum is thinning out and leadership is narrowing.

Sector rotation is flashing classic late-cycle signals. Tech (XLK, $180.39) still leads, but Materials (XLB), Healthcare (XLV), Staples (XLP), and Financials (XLF) are all showing improving momentum—rotational plays are back on the table. Industrials (XLI) and Communications (XLC) are lagging hard—avoid the canaries. Watch Financials (XLF): $51.94 just above the 20 MA ($51.59), so nothing broken yet, but sickly sectors can turn fast.

Contrarian radar: No extreme sentiment triggers today, but DELL’s wild 16.8% pump and several high-flyers (ARM, AA, SM) look stretched—if you missed those, abundance mindset: don’t chase, wait for mean reversion back to the weekly 20 MA. Bonds aren’t freaking out (HYG flat, LQD inching up), so the macro structure holds. Weakness in commodities (GLD, SLV, CL=F) is worth noting—flows out of hard assets, back into risk. Keep watching dark pool levels and MAs: if we get a daily close below 20 MA on majors, hit the exit button fast.
callout_body:
SPY: $745.64, 20 MA $731.58 — uptrend intact
XLK: $180.39, leadership, momentum strong
XLF: $51.94 (watch for 20 MA break)
DELL: $295.19 (+16.8%), way above 20 MA ($234.41) — don't chase
HYG: $79.91 (macro canary)
VIX: 16.78, regime = contraction
action_items: Rotate into Healthcare (XLV), Materials (XLB), and Staples (XLP) if you need edges—momentum/relative strength is peaking.
Set alerts for daily closes below 20 MA on SPY, XLK, XLF. If the break comes, reset risk immediately.
closing_quote: If it's in the press, it's in the price. Patience, react, don't predict.
